basic_details.job_title: Rajasthan Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for Anganwadi Worker & Helper Posts in Alwar District
basic_details.department: Women and Child Development Department (WCD), Alwar
basic_details.post_name: Honorary / voluntary remuneration service (not a regular salaried government post)
basic_details.application_mode: Offline only - submit directly to the concerned Project Officer's office
basic_details.short_information: The Office of the Deputy Director, Women and Child Development Department (ICDS), Alwar has invited offline applications for vacant Anganwadi Karyakarta (Worker) and Sahayika (Helper) posts under Notice No. 01/2026-27, dated June 27, 2026. These are honorary/voluntary remuneration positions (स्वैच्छिक मानदेय सेवा), filled locally without a written examination, across 10 Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) projects in Alwar district.

age_limit.details: The maximum age limit for applicants is 35 years. Age relaxation provisions, where applicable based on prior recognized Anganwadi-related service, are subject to departmental rules.

qualification.details: The applicant must be a local resident of the specific revenue village (in rural areas) or ward (in urban areas) that falls within the catchment area of the Anganwadi center for which she is applying. A widowed or divorced woman may be considered a local resident based on either her parental or marital residence, whichever applies. | Both married women and women with special eligibility status (such as those belonging to Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, widowed, divorced, or other specified special categories) are eligible to apply for Anganwadi Karyakarta and Sahayika posts. | The applicant must have passed Class 12 (12th standard) as the minimum educational qualification. | The maximum age limit for applicants is 35 years. Age relaxation provisions, where applicable based on prior recognized Anganwadi-related service, are subject to departmental rules. | In Anganwadi center catchment areas where Scheduled Caste, minority, or Scheduled Tribe population constitutes 50% or more of the local population, preference in selection will be given to a woman belonging to that category. In other areas, selection will generally be based on the Anganwadi center's registered population base.

how_to_apply: Obtain the application form free of cost from the concerned Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) office listed in the vacancy table above.
Fill the application form completely with all required details.
Submit the completed application, along with all necessary supporting documents, in person or via email to the concerned project office. Applications sent by registered post will not be accepted.
Ensure you obtain an acknowledgment/receipt for your submitted application.
Submit your application on or before the last date, July 29, 2026.
important_instructions: Applications must be submitted only to the specific project office corresponding to the Anganwadi center applied for. Email or registered-post applications are not accepted - only in-person or the project's specified email submission (where indicated) is valid. No modification or correction to the application will be accepted once the last date for submission has passed. The complete detailed procedure for filling honorary Anganwadi posts follows Departmental Circular No. 4244 dated 12/01/2023 and the revised guidelines dated 01/03/2023. Selection is based on eligibility, local residency, and category norms - there is no written examination for these posts.

rows.details: Women and Child Development Department (WCD), Alwar
Honorary / voluntary remuneration service (not a regular salaried government post)
Offline only - submit directly to the concerned Project Officer's office

content_sections.heading: Vacant Posts by ICDS Project
content_sections.text: Vacant Anganwadi Karyakarta and Sahayika posts have been notified across 10 Child Development Project offices in Alwar district. Applicants must apply to the specific project office covering their local Anganwadi center. Note: The original notification lists specific Anganwadi center names, ward numbers, and category reservations (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/MI) for each vacant post under every project. Due to scan quality, these center-level details should be verified directly against the source document before publishing - see the note to the editorial team below.

rows.project_office_email: ICDS.THENI (email not clearly legible in source)

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: The applicant must be a local resident of the specific revenue village (in rural areas) or ward (in urban areas) that falls within the catchment area of the Anganwadi center for which she is applying. A widowed or divorced woman may be considered a local resident based on either her parental or marital residence, whichever applies. Both married women and women with special eligibility status (such as those belonging to Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, widowed, divorced, or other specified special categories) are eligible to apply for Anganwadi Karyakarta and Sahayika posts. The applicant must have passed Class 12 (12th standard) as the minimum educational qualification. The maximum age limit for applicants is 35 years. Age relaxation provisions, where applicable based on prior recognized Anganwadi-related service, are subject to departmental rules. In Anganwadi center catchment areas where Scheduled Caste, minority, or Scheduled Tribe population constitutes 50% or more of the local population, preference in selection will be given to a woman belonging to that category. In other areas, selection will generally be based on the Anganwadi center's registered population base.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Obtain the application form free of cost from the concerned Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) office listed in the vacancy table above. Fill the application form completely with all required details. Submit the completed application, along with all necessary supporting documents, in person or via email to the concerned project office. Applications sent by registered post will not be accepted. Ensure you obtain an acknowledgment/receipt for your submitted application. Submit your application on or before the last date, July 29, 2026.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted only to the specific project office corresponding to the Anganwadi center applied for. Email or registered-post applications are not accepted - only in-person or the project's specified email submission (where indicated) is valid. No modification or correction to the application will be accepted once the last date for submission has passed. The complete detailed procedure for filling honorary Anganwadi posts follows Departmental Circular No. 4244 dated 12/01/2023 and the revised guidelines dated 01/03/2023. Selection is based on eligibility, local residency, and category norms - there is no written examination for these posts.

faq.question: Q1. Is there a written exam for Alwar Anganwadi Karyakarta/Sahayika recruitment?
faq.answer: No. Selection is made locally based on eligibility, local residency, and category-based criteria as per departmental guidelines — there is no written examination.

faq.question: Q2. What is the minimum qualification for Anganwadi Karyakarta or Sahayika?
faq.answer: Applicants must have passed Class 12 as the minimum educational qualification.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for these posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 35 years, as specified in the notification.

faq.question: Q4. Can I apply for any Anganwadi center in Alwar district?
faq.answer: No. You may only apply for a post in the Anganwadi center whose catchment village (rural) or ward (urban) is your place of local residence.

faq.question: Q5. What is the last date to apply?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application to the concerned project office is July 29, 2026.

faq.question: Q6. Can I submit my application by post or email?
faq.answer: No. The notification states that applications sent by registered post will not be accepted; submission must be made in person or via the specified email of the concerned project office.

faq.question: Q7. Is there an application fee?
faq.answer: The application form is available free of cost from the concerned project office; no fee is mentioned in the notification.
